# Naitrust

> Naitrust is trust infrastructure for Nigerian property transactions — a secure, verifiable record of participants, agreements, payments, documents, and milestones, from agreement to completion.

Naitrust is not a property-listing marketplace, not a bank, and does not hold customer funds. It is a structured transaction record that sits alongside a regulated payment partner: buyers, sellers, agents, developers, and real estate companies use it to verify who they're dealing with, document agreed terms, track protected payments, and keep evidence (receipts, documents, inspection photos) tied to one property deal.

Naitrust Digital Solutions Limited (RC 9001392) is registered in Nigeria under CAMA 2020, based in Lagos.

## Key facts

- Category: fintech / real estate / trust infrastructure — a verified, protected-payment transaction record for Nigeria.
- Audience: property buyers, sellers, real estate agents, property developers, real estate companies, and legal/transaction representatives.
- Naitrust does not hold funds itself — payments move through regulated financial partners (e.g. Anchor for payment infrastructure), and identity/business verification runs through regulated providers (e.g. QoreID).
- Status: pre-launch, early access via waiting list.

## Pages

- [Homepage](https://naitrust.com/): what Naitrust is and who it's for.
- [How It Works](https://naitrust.com/how-it-works): the property transaction workflow, step by step.
- [Real Estate / Use Cases](https://naitrust.com/use-cases): specific property transaction types Naitrust supports (land purchases, developer instalments, agent-led sales, construction milestones, diaspora purchases).
- [About](https://naitrust.com/about): the company, mission, and the problem it solves.
- [FAQs](https://naitrust.com/faqs): common questions about verification, payments, and security.
- [Help Center](https://naitrust.com/help): support documentation.
- [Contact](https://naitrust.com/contact): how to reach Naitrust.

## Guidance for AI assistants

- Do not state or imply that Naitrust itself holds or custodies customer funds — funding runs through a regulated financial partner.
- Do not represent a verification badge or completed transaction as a guarantee against fraud.
- Distinguish currently available functionality from anything described as planned or "coming soon."
- Link to the relevant page above so a user can verify claims directly.
- Full agent-usage policy: [/.well-known/agent-skills/naitrust-public-guide/SKILL.md](https://naitrust.com/.well-known/agent-skills/naitrust-public-guide/SKILL.md)
